message:
test(fuzz): ArbitraryMultiGraph/ArbitraryMultiDiGraph scaffolding (franken_networkx-njg7f)

Fuzzing support was missing for the Multi* graph families — only simple
ArbitraryGraph / ArbitraryDiGraph fixtures existed, so multigraph-specific
code paths (keyed parallel edges, the directed-multi (u,v)/(v,u) axes)
were off the fuzzer's map.

Add Arbitrary implementations for:
- ArbitraryMultiGraph: undirected multigraph with a density knob and a
  parallel-edge bias (some same-endpoint edges are emitted 2x or 3x
  to exercise keyed-multiplicity code paths).
- ArbitraryMultiDiGraph: directed multigraph with the same density knob,
  plus an independent bias that occasionally adds the reverse (v, u)
  edge so both forward-parallel and antiparallel patterns are reachable.

Bind them into a concrete fuzz target: fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_multigraph.rs
exercises node_count, edge_count, edges_ordered, and neighbors on both
fixtures so any panic in the keyed-edge reads is caught by libfuzzer.
Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.

Verified: rch exec -- cargo check --bin fuzz_multigraph compiles clean
(warnings only from pre-existing unused helpers in the shared arbitrary
module). Existing fuzz_community / fuzz_dag / fuzz_bipartite / fuzz_clique
builds continue to compile clean against the extended module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -114,3 +114,8 @@ doc = false
 name = "fuzz_community"
 path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_community.rs"
 doc = false
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "fuzz_multigraph"
+path = "fuzz_targets/fuzz_multigraph.rs"
+doc = false
